title: Chapter Sixty-Four: The Blood Flame Sect Leader
content:
## JEONG YEON-SHIN

For two days the Seventh Apostle fed him porridge with her own hands.

Finely chopped meat. A ceramic bowl. She held it to his lips and would not let servants near. Her single red eye watched him eat with the rapt attention of a collector handling something irreplaceable.

*She treats me like a pet.*

The thought should have been humiliating. Instead Yeon-shin used it. Let her feed him. Let her believe she held something precious and tame. Every mouthful was strength returning to his body, the sword song's backlash fading, his sealed pressure points slowly loosening under the blood technique qi that lurked in his meridians like poison.

On the third day, she unsealed him.

Not fully. She planted blood technique qi at critical meridian points -- a leash disguised as a gift. The qi would cause fatal internal injury if not periodically removed. By her.

"Let's go pay respects to the Sect Leader," she said with a slight smile.

Yeon-shin rose silently. First outing.

---

The Blood Flame Sect's main hall was a city.

He had not expected that. Classical halls arranged in orderly fashion, hundreds of people passing in all directions, most with crimson hair. A developed village hidden somewhere within a fortnight's travel from Huizhou.

He memorised everything. Streets, structures, patrol routes, the composition of masters. Blood Swordmasters lowered their gaze as the Seventh Apostle passed. Regular members did not dare look at all.

Then one swordmaster saw him and dropped to one knee.

"Great Teacher!"

Yeon-shin recognised him. One of the two who had thrown thunderclap bombs at the Dragon-Phoenix Gathering.

The man was not bowing to the Apostle. He was bowing to Yeon-shin.

"This unworthy sect swordsman respectfully greets the Grand Teacher."

Silence.

*This is not the treatment of a prisoner.*

The Seventh Apostle's eye curved with pleasure. "I announced it widely," she said. "Your track record, your creative talent, your character. What would happen if we plant blood techniques in your body? Our sect might see the birth of a small sect leader after a hundred some years."

"Grand Teacher!" More were gathering now. Red hair on every side. "Perfect the divine blood techniques!"

Madmen. Yeon-shin thought the word clearly, deliberately. They called their martial arts divine while demanding he rewrite the verses. They spoke of overcoming heaven's punishment while asking him to change their hair. Contradiction heaped on contradiction, and not one of them noticed.

He said nothing. Lowered his gaze. Let them chant.

When the crowd dispersed, the Apostle gripped his sleeve with fingers dense with true qi and led him forward.

"Let's go. My small sect leader. My Grand Teacher."

---

The Sect Leader's hall rose above the city like a palace. Thirty-six steps leading up to a crimson gate.

*Thirty-six. Taoism's evil stars. Buddhism's afflictions.*

He counted them while pretending not to, engraving every detail of enemy territory in his eyes. Every stone. Every beam. Every joint where mortar met wood.

The gate opened on its own.

"Come closer."

A white-haired man sat on a high throne. Handsome. Noble features like imperial family. One leg draped across the wide seat with the indolence of a man who had never been refused anything. No energy emanated from him at all.

*Like the Sect Lord.*

The comparison came unbidden, and Yeon-shin pushed it away. The absence of detectable qi in a supreme master was not peace. It was control so absolute that even energy sense could find nothing to grip.

The Third Apostle -- a middle-aged man with pitch-black hair -- stood among the ranked masters, spirit wrapped around him like armour. His tone was overbearing.

"The Sect Leader has spoken. Did you not hear?"

"Is there an Apostle above Grand Teacher?" Yeon-shin asked without changing his tone.

The Seventh Apostle burst into laughter. Ecstatic. Unhinged. She did not stop even before the Sect Leader's throne.

"Disgusting," the Third Apostle said. "This Desolate Sect mongrel claims to be our Grand Teacher. His position isn't even decided yet."

The Sect Leader raised a languid hand. His snow-white hair shifted. A breeze swept the hall and every scrap of energy -- the Third Apostle's menace, the Seventh Apostle's bewitching edge -- was erased in an instant.

Like blowing out a candle.

"You speak of trite matters," the Sect Leader said.

Then the Third Apostle extended one arm. Blood technique energy rose from his palm -- red qi layering into a circular barrier, shimmering with sharpness. Attack and defence unified in a wall of pure internal energy.

"The test our rising talents take when promoted to Blood Swordmaster," the Seventh Apostle whispered in Yeon-shin's ear. "They say spirit medicine is granted even for enduring one breath."

"What happens if I tear it?"

He stepped forward. The Demon-Destroying Azure Unyielding Palm flowed through his right hand -- clear energy spreading outward, blue light racing through a cultivation path that read the barrier's architecture and unravelled it verse by verse.

The red wall shattered.

Wind swept the hall. Red energy scattered like torn cloth.

Silence.

"...If it's truly a martial art created by him," the Third Apostle said slowly, stepping back. His eyes held something sharp and calculating. "Truly a dangerous fellow."

The Blood Flame Sect Leader spoke into the quiet.

"That power. That true qi operation method." He smiled faintly. "Learning peerless martial arts through verses alone? Great sects would have no reason to watch for talent when taking in disciples. Your technique goes further. I feel an independent interpretation of dharma power melted in. Mental focus is important -- that is why one needs a master."

He let the words settle.

"You alone were the problem. And now you've come to our sect."

He clapped once.

A servant appeared bearing a tray draped in golden silk. Upon it, a classical box. Inside the box, a pill the colour of congealed blood.

"What is it?"

"A poison that narrows the meridians. Our sect's secret art."

Devil nature in his smile. A bewitching, ancient thing.

Yeon-shin stepped to the throne and took the pill from the Sect Leader's open palm. Crimson-black. Heavy. He turned it in his fingers.

"How can one live with blocked qi and blood? This kills people."

"It would be useful when rising to the Apostle realm. Cultivate your meridians through true qi. You should be able to endure it."

*Trust this man? Nonsense.*

He tucked the pill into his breast. Compliance bought time. Time bought survival.

The Sect Leader clapped again. Two books on a servant's hands.

"Demon Roaring Blood Technique. I bestow the Apostle's secret arts to the Grand Teacher."

Yeon-shin took the manuals. The paper felt new. Recently transcribed.

*Blood Flame Sect Apostle cultivation technique. In my hands.*

He pictured blood running down those thirty-six steps. Upper dantian precognition, or imagination -- he could not tell. His crown throbbed.

He turned and walked out without bowing.

"Show some respect!" the Third Apostle snarled behind him. Momentum surged, then died -- the Sect Leader had quelled it with a look.

The Seventh Apostle caught up and linked arms, her face rapturous.

"I'm so excited about the reborn blood techniques. How long will it take?"

He did not answer. He was already counting steps, memorising corridors, measuring distances.

Sharpening the blade in his heart.

---
